title: Upper City Cantina
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}

{{Quote|You aren't from Taris, are you? Do yourself a favor and stay away from the Tarisian ale—it packs more kick than most off-worlders can handle.|Tarisian patron|<ref name="kotor" />}}
The '''Upper City Cantina''' was a drinking and dining establishment located in the [[Upper City]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] was a place where many [[Taris/Legends|Tarisians]], including [[Noble/Legends|nobles]], local merchants, [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]] players, and well-off [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]], enjoyed [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]-famous [[Tarisian ale]], as well as the famous [[Taris dueling ring|dueling ring]] located there. During the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire's]] occupation of that world at the height of the [[Jedi Civil War]], many off-duty [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] and officers from the nearby [[Tarisian military base|Tarisian Military Base]] frequently visited the establishment along with its regular customers and offworlders trapped on the planet because of the quarantine, which was aimed at preventing the escape of [[Bastila Shan]]. While in search of a way to rescue the missing [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] officer [[Carth Onasi]] and the amnesiac former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] visited the cantina in hopes of gathering information on Taris. The establishment, along with much of the surface of Taris, was destroyed as a result of the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that had been ordered by [[Darth Malak]].

==Layout==
{{Quote|Isn't this band great? They can't leave Taris because of the quarantine, so they've been playing every night! Talk about a good break for us!|Tarisian citizen|<ref name="kotor" />}}
[[File:UpperCantinaEntrance.png|thumb|left|180px|The cantina's main entrance, guarded by a [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith trooper]]]]
The Upper City Cantina featured an expansive layout, which consisted of four rooms arranged around a central core, as well as a [[Taris dueling ring|dueling ring]] that was located elsewhere within the building. As a visitor entered the establishment, they were met by a collection of [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]] tables where local players would engage in the popular card game. In addition to its prominent bar area, the common area featured an array of tables for guests to sit at; this room also served as a central hub for the rest of the establishment's various rooms.<ref name="kotor" />

The room immediately to the left of the entrance and the area dedicated to pazaak was used by [[Ajuur]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] to organize sanctioned duels between amateur or professional combatants who typically competed for a share of the winnings earned by Ajuur through gambling. The duelists who frequented the cantina included [[Gerlon Two-Fingers]], [[Marl]], [[Ice (duelist)|Ice]], [[Deadeye Duncan]], and the champions [[Twitch (Rodian)|Twitch]] and [[Gorse Bendak|Bendak Starkiller]]. When not engaged in organized competition, the combatants would often congregate in this area; some would converse with various patrons. In addition, several [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreens]] were set up for spectators who were unable to secure tickets to the duels themselves but still wanted to watch the fights. The duel ring itself was circular, with only one entrance; it was surrounded on all sides by stands that could hold a number of spectators. The ring itself was equipped with energy suppressors, so that despite the unrestricted choice of weapons offered the combatants, the chances of being killed in the typical match were very small.<ref name="kotor" />

[[File:UCCan_layout.jpg|thumb|200px|The cantina's basic layout]]
The room of the cantina to the right of the entryway, which was a relatively new addition, contained a bandstand and biological or holographic dancers, which were usually in the form of [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]]. An assortment of tables filled the rest of the room, providing seating for patrons to watch the performers. The final room, which was located directly opposite the entrance into the main area, was laid out as a more private place in which to enjoy drinks and meals. Tarisian nobles would often congregate there, served by wait staff.<ref name="kotor" />

==History==
In the years before the [[Tarisian Civil War|Taris Civil War]], a century of prosperity saw the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] main cities grow so rapidly that they soon covered all available landmasses; it was during this era that the Upper City Cantina was built. The newly formed [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] became divided into levels; the [[Upper City]] became home to the wealthy and the nobility, the [[Lower City]], began to fall into disrepair and decay; and the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]], where those who were banished for committing crimes against the government attempted to dredge out a meager existence. When new [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace routes]] were discovered that marginalized Taris's importance as a trading hub, the planet lost much of its income. The civil war that followed pitted the oppressed underclasses, as well as the many [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that called the world their home, against the rich and the corrupt planetary government. The conflict proved devastating, killing millions and leaving great swaths of the planet in shambles or simply abandoned. Many more people were banished to the Undercity, while the aliens of Taris were relegated to the Lower City, where they often fell prey to [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] or other criminals.<ref>Based on planetary history as told by [[Rukil]] to the player character upon meeting him in the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]].</ref>

===The Jedi Civil War===
[[File:UCCan_musicroom.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The music room of the cantina, featuring [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] musicians and [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] dancers]]
{{Quote|It's a good thing a few of the Sith come in here to relax when they're off-duty. That should keep them from closing this cantina down.|Tarisian patron|<ref name="kotor" />}}
The establishment itself, however, was largely unaffected by the conflict, as it continued to serve Tarisian ale and play host to [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] competitions. In [[3956 BBY]], during the height of the [[Jedi Civil War]], the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] under [[Darth Malak]] conquered the planet in preparation for an [[Attack on the Endar Spire|ambush]] on a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] supporting a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Warship/Legends|warship]], the ''[[Endar Spire]]''; the battle was intended to capture the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bastila Shan]] in order to use her [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to ensure the success of his campaign. In the wake of the conquest, the cantina would serve as a place of entertainment for the many troops and officers of the occupation force. Unknown to Malak, however, his former friend and [[Sith Master/Legends|master]], whom the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] had [[Capture of Darth Revan|attempted]] to kill [[3957 BBY|one year previously]], was also aboard the ship. Bastila and the amnesiac [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], along with [[Carth Onasi]], were able to escape the doomed vessel; owing to their abandoning ship in different [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], the group became separated, with Shan ending up in the Undercity, where she was quickly captured by a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] known as the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]]. Carth and Revan, meanwhile, sought refuge in an abandoned apartment not far from the cantina and their pod's crash site, where the former [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] was rendered [[Coma/Legends|comatose]] for three days. Subsequent to the failure to capture or kill Shan in the immediate battle, Malak imposed a planetwide blockade.<ref name="kotor" />

{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
After recovering, Revan and Carth visited the cantina to learn about the situation on Taris, conferring with a number of patrons, including [[Sarna (Sith military)|Sarna]], who invited Revan to a party elsewhere in the Upper City. While at the cantina, Revan expressed an interest in entering the dueling competition being run by Ajuur, which he did under the moniker of the "[[Mysterious Stranger]]" in order to raise [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to help find passage offworld. Eventually, after [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|further exploration and involvement in other incidents]], including winning the [[Tarisian Season Opener]] [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop race]], the pair, now joined by Bastila, met with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Canderous Ordo]] at the cantina. Ordo, who had grown dissatisfied with working for [[Davik Kang]], had developed a plan for breaking the Sith [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] and leaving the planet. Hoping to bring Revan, Carth and Bastila in to help carry it out, the mercenary offered them a deal: in exchange for obtaining the launch codes necessary to allow a ship to leave the planet unmolested by the Sith fleet, he would provide them with the means of escape—access to Davik's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''. With the codes in their possession, the team met up with Ordo at another establishment, [[Javyar's Cantina|Javyar's]] in the Lower City, for the next step in the plan.<ref name="kotor" />
{{Endgame}}

===Destruction===
{{Quote|The search for Bastila is taking too long. Destroy the entire planet.|Darth Malak|<ref name="kotor" />}}
[[File:UCCan_backroom.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The back room of the cantina]]
As this was happening, however, Malak grew impatient with the search, which was becoming increasingly fruitless. As a last-ditch attempt to prevent Shan from escaping the planet, he ordered [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] to conduct an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. After several hours, during which the fleet was repositioned in order to carry out the action with the highest degree of efficiency, the bombardment commenced; [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire raked the city-world without mercy<ref name="kotor" /> as automated landers descended to the surface to pick up Sith personnel who were still stationed planetside.<ref name="cut">[http://www.team-gizka.org Team Gizka Project Website]; Forum postings discussing content that was cut from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' and later "restored" indicate this as an explanation for why [[Deadeye Duncan]] was able to escape the conflagration.</ref> The cantina was destroyed by the thorough bombardment.<ref name="kotor" />

==Inhabitants==
[[File:UCCan_pazaakroom.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]] room at the cantina entrance]]
{{Quote|It's bad enough the Sith conquered this planet, but do they have to come in here to relax and rub their presence in our faces?|Tarisian citizen|<ref name="kotor" />}}
Many individuals from the Upper City of Taris frequented the cantina, including nobles, well-to-do merchants, off-world traders and other citizens. In keeping with the local tradition of [[Humanocentrism]], the place catered almost exclusively to [[Human/Legends|Human]] clientèle, as members of non-Human [[Species/Legends|species]] were generally not allowed to live or work on this highest tier of the ecumenopolis without first obtaining a special permit. The only exceptions to this policy at that time were the reigning dueling champion of that year, a Rodian named Twitch; a trio of Bith musicians who had been stranded on the planet; and a pair of Twi'lek dancers. Other notable denizens included the card shark [[Niklos]]; an older pazaak player named [[Garouk]]; [[Gana Lavin]], a young noblewoman and her friend, and the duel organizer Ajuur the Hutt. Other patrons included various nobles and citizens; during the quarantine of Taris in the wake of the Sith conquest, patronage was further bolstered by the presence of Sith personnel and off-worlders trapped on the planet because of the blockade. Local reaction to these developments ranged from carefully hidden resentment to open-hearted welcome; some Sith, however, fell prey to the unusually potent nature of Tarisian ale.<ref name="kotor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Upper City Cantina was first introduced as a playable sub-level of the Taris Upper City in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. The player character has a number of alternatives when it comes to the Upper City Cantina. He or she can choose to participate in an illegal [[Death/Legends|death]] match against Bendak Starkiller for [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] points, or choose to ignore the dueling ring entirely; despite this, the introductory duel cinematic between Gerlon Two-Fingers and Deadeye Duncan will automatically run when the player enters the duel room. In addition, depending on the gender of the player character, they can be invited to a party, where they can gain a set of [[Sith trooper armor/Legends|Sith armor]] without resorting to force, by either [[Sarna (Sith military)|Sarna]] if the player is male, or [[Yun Genda]] if the player is female; this too is optional.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

In cut content, it was possible for Deadeye Duncan to have survived the destruction of Taris; during an interactive cutscene between him and the player on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]], he will ask to use the Mysterious Stranger alias. The player was to have the option of questioning him on how he escaped the bombardment, during which Duncan admits that he found an automated transport whose intended passengers had been killed by fallen debris.<ref name="cut" />
